title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has released a policy document outlining its response to a 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards. The document outlines plans to address concerns raised in the report, aiming to ensure the development and use of AI align with public expectations and values.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of using copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. It evaluates the implications of copyright law on the creation and use of AI, likely highlighting potential issues and recommendations for improvement.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a policy in place regarding the use of generative artificial intelligence in education. The policy outlines the department's position on this technology to ensure its safe and effective integration into educational settings.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The DfT's Transport AI Action Plan is a document that outlines how they will use artificial intelligence technology in the transport sector. It aims to balance the benefits of AI with potential risks, by working with the transport industry.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This progress report outlines the use of copyrighted works in the development of AI systems as per the Data (Use and Access) Act. It aims to provide updates on the implementation of AI copyright policies and their impact on the development of AI technologies.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector, a field that was requested for guidance by several government ministers. It provides an overview of how Ofqual will oversee the development and implementation of AI in educational qualifications.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is developing a policy to regulate artificial intelligence used in medical devices, based on recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council. This policy aims to ensure the safety and reliability of AI-driven medical devices.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on Artificial Intelligence, 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'. The response aims to outline the government's plans and actions to address the committee's recommendations on AI development and regulation in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and public standards. Its main purpose is to provide guidelines and frequently asked questions for the committee to consider during their review.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided written evidence to a parliamentary inquiry about how to regulate Artificial Intelligence. The submission aims to inform the development of effective governance for AI in the UK.
